meta.title: Sunder Next — Post-Migration Product Roadmap
meta.updated: 2026-04-14: Added ws-13 (Enforced skill bundles + editable skills), ws-14 (Third-party skill marketplace — markdown + env pattern, inspired by Gooseworks/athina-ai model), ws-15 (Memory consolidation background agents), ws-16 (Multi-agent router / specialist pattern).
meta.context: Sunder moved from a custom orchestration loop (streamText + tool dispatch + compaction) to Anthropic Managed Agents. This roadmap tracks everything built on that new foundation. The v2 plan is frozen history — any remaining v2 items that still matter are carried forward here.

conventions.ids: Workstreams use ws-{number}. Tasks use ws-{number}-{task}. No more PR numbers.
conventions.tasklists: Each workstream links to a standalone tasklist in docs/tasks/. The tasklist is the source of truth for implementation detail. This file tracks scope, status, and dependencies.

workstreams.title: Managed Agents — Official Pattern Refactor
workstreams.goal: Refactor chat pipeline to long-lived session subscription + fire-and-forget messages. Drop the one-POST-per-turn adapter.
workstreams.notes: Continuation of the managed agents migration (docs/product/plans/2026-04-09-001-feat-managed-agents-migration-plan.md). Foundation for everything else.

workstreams.title: Native MCP Connections — Replace Composio
workstreams.goal: Replace Composio middleware with native MCP server integrations via Anthropic Managed Agents. User completes OAuth, token stored in Anthropic vault, agent authenticates automatically via MCP.
workstreams.notes: Hybrid migration: start with Google suite (Gmail, Calendar, Drive), keep Composio as fallback for long-tail integrations. Eliminates ~7 custom connection tools, Composio SDK dependency, and the execute_composio_tool middleware hop.

workstreams.title: Browser Chat Rework
workstreams.goal: Rework chat UI for the new session model — SSE stream, optimistic messages, interrupt support.

workstreams.title: Chat Latency Optimization
workstreams.goal: Reduce time-to-first-token and overall response latency in the managed agents pipeline.

workstreams.title: Skills Migration to Managed Agents
workstreams.goal: Migrate instruction skills from Supabase Storage filesystem to Anthropic Managed Agents skills API.

workstreams.title: Enforced Skill Bundles + Editable Skills
workstreams.goal: Move from soft-gated managed-agent skills to Sunder-owned enforced skill loading, bundle shadowing, forks, and user-created skills.
workstreams.notes: Current production path remains the shipped soft-gated version: all catalog skills are attached to the managed agent, install state is communicated via context engineering, and the system prompt + skill descriptions act as the short-term gate. This workstream is the clean follow-on for true enforcement. Skills remain folder bundles (`SKILL.md`, `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/`). Runtime should discover via SQL metadata, lazily hydrate only the selected bundle into the session filesystem, and resolve shadowing with private > shared > public precedence. Future client-created/forked skills should be authored through `skill-creator`, not raw markdown editing.

workstreams.title: Third-Party Skill Marketplace — Markdown + Env Pattern
workstreams.goal: Enable third-party skill integration where a skill is a markdown document (instructions, endpoints, auth pattern) plus environment credentials. Agent executes via existing HTTP/browser tools — no custom tool code required per integration.
workstreams.notes: Insight from Gooseworks/athina-ai LinkedIn Scraper: their 'tool' is just a README telling the LLM how to call an API with a Bearer token. Generalize this — a skill catalog entry is markdown instructions + env var references. Agent reads the skill doc at session start, credentials are injected as env vars. Adding a new third-party capability becomes: write markdown, set env var, redeploy agent definition. No tool handler code. Depends on ws-13 (enforced skill bundles) for the skill loading infrastructure.

workstreams.title: Presigned Uploads
workstreams.goal: Replace direct uploads with presigned S3 flow for reliability and larger file support.

workstreams.title: Chat Artifact Downloads
workstreams.goal: Allow users to download files the agent generates during a session.

workstreams.title: Unified File Reference Model
workstreams.goal: Single FileRef type across uploads, agent outputs, and CRM attachments.

workstreams.title: Automations UX Overhaul
workstreams.goal: Redesign triggers/automations page for clarity and usability.

workstreams.title: Billing Page
workstreams.goal: Stripe-backed billing and subscription management in settings.

workstreams.title: Chat Context Engineering Cleanup
workstreams.goal: Clean up system prompt assembly, context injection, and reminder patterns for the managed agents model.

workstreams.title: CRM Tool Efficiency
workstreams.goal: Reduce token cost and improve accuracy of CRM tool calls.

workstreams.title: Memory Consolidation Background Agents
workstreams.goal: Nightly background agent pass per active client that curates SOUL.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md, and memory/*.md files — merging duplicates, promoting/demoting tiers, flagging stale entries, preserving corrections.
workstreams.notes: Two approaches to decide in planning: (A) single-agent consolidation pass (cheaper, faster to ship), (B) three-agent panel — Consolidator + Adversarial + Judge (richer curation, higher token cost). Corrections are never deleted unless superseded. No user-facing UI in v1. Triggered by existing Trigger.dev cron. Requirements: docs/product/ideations/2026-04-14-multi-agent-architecture-requirements.md (Part B).

workstreams.title: Multi-Agent Router / Specialist Pattern
workstreams.goal: Redesign the main Sunder agent as a coordinator that delegates to pre-declared specialist agents — CRM, Research, Drafting, Meeting, Automation — each with a minimal tool set and focused system prompt.
workstreams.notes: Blocked on Anthropic Managed Agents Research Preview access (apply at claude.com/form/claude-managed-agents). Architecture is fully designed: specialists are pre-declared via callable_agents on the orchestrator agent definition. One level of delegation only (platform constraint). Specialists share the session container/filesystem but run in context-isolated threads. Requirements: docs/product/ideations/2026-04-14-multi-agent-architecture-requirements.md (Part A).

carried_from_v2.note: Items from the v2 plan that are still relevant but not yet started. Will be promoted to workstreams when active.

items.title: Observability / eval harness
items.note: D4 in managed agents plan dropped Langfuse. Needs new approach — Anthropic Console + in-process evaluators.
